mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits through lower discount rates and easier financial conditions, lifting equity valuations and pushing S&P 500 returns positive over the next month when the policy path is already in an easing phase.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not positive in a majority of months with falling FEDFUNDS, or if the rolling 3-year hit rate falls below 55%, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS crosses above an elevated policy threshold, tighter discounting and recession risk reprice equities downward, so S&P 500 forward returns over the next month turn negative after the threshold breach.
failure_condition: Reject if post-threshold 20-day S&P 500 returns are positive more than 50% of the time, or if the thresholded signal has Rank IC above 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE indicates weakening household income and slower demand, which compresses earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next month when unemployment is moving higher on a 3-month basis.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not negative in rising-UNRATE episodes, or if the signal's sign reverses for two consecutive years in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in PAYEMS paired with rising RSAFS signals broadening payroll income and retail demand, which supports earnings revisions and pushes S&P 500 returns higher over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-RSAFS improvement does not outperform either series alone in predicting 60-day returns, or if the combined signal has a hit rate below 55% over any 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO together with improving HOUST indicates an upturn in real activity and capacity utilization, which raises cyclical earnings expectations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous gains in INDPRO and HOUST do not precede positive 20-day S&P 500 returns more often than chance, or if the interaction term has no incremental predictive power versus single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: If PAYEMS contracts while UNRATE rises, labor-market deterioration signals falling future consumption and margin pressure, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls and UNRATE rises over the same month
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS decline and UNRATE increase does not produce negative 5-day returns in at least 60% of triggered cases, or if the effect disappears after controlling for the individual series changes.

mechanism_narrative: In a disinflationary regime, a falling CPIAUCSL combined with flat-to-easing FEDFUNDS lowers real rates and supports risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 60-day S&P 500 returns are not concentrated in periods of falling CPIAUCSL and non-rising FEDFUNDS, or if the joint condition fails to outperform either proxy alone.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth is positive but retail sales fail to confirm with RSAFS contraction, the economy is losing demand momentum beneath the surface and equities should weaken over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the divergence between GDPC1 and RSAFS does not precede negative 20-day returns more often than positive returns, or if the divergence signal has no incremental value in out-of-sample testing.
